patch.value: Relationship timestamp snapshots must honor event time for late and offline arrivals

RelationshipEventLog.snapshot({atTimestamp}) currently chooses an append prefix ending at the last event whose timestamp is <= the target. If a later-appended offline event has an older event-time, the prefix can include earlier-appended events whose timestamps are after the requested instant. Point-in-time reconstruction must filter all immutable events by event time while preserving append order among included events.

Timestamp snapshots exclude every event after the requested event-time regardless of append order; late and offline arrivals remain visible at their actual event-time; version snapshots and current snapshots retain append-sequence semantics; regression tests cover add, supersede, remove, empty, and out-of-order streams; SDK docs state append-time versus event-time semantics
Timestamp snapshots materialize exactly the relationships effective from events at or before the requested instant, including late arrivals and excluding future-dated events.
The append-prefix algorithm can include future-dated events when a later append carries an older timestamp.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-23: all-status searches for relationship snapshot timestamp, offline late event, out-of-order event-time, and point-in-time relationship reads found the closed relationship platform pm-ju83, the active digital-twin exemplar pm-kr3t, and generic event-stream/federation items, but no item tracking this exact append-prefix correctness defect.

author: codex-root
message: Implement event-time-correct relationship snapshots with digital-twin regression coverage

value.note: late and offline arrival regression coverage

value.author: codex-root
value.text: TDD evidence: the regression appends future event-time data before late/offline historical data, supersedes and removes relationships, and asserts point-in-time snapshots select all events at or before the boundary while retaining the highest included append sequence. Focused tests and the 6129-test 100% coverage suite pass.

patch.value: Fixed event-time snapshot reconstruction for out-of-order late/offline relationship events; focused regression and full 100% coverage suite pass, and SDK semantics are documented.

author: codex-root

patch.value: Late and offline events appear at their true event time; future-dated events are excluded even when appended earlier; current/version snapshots retain append semantics; regression and full coverage gates pass.
RelationshipEventLog timestamp snapshots now filter every immutable event by event time, preserve append order among included events, and report the highest included append sequence.

message: Complete closeout metadata after verified fix
